evals.prompt: Scan this Go service for observability gaps. The code is in examples/go/chi-basic/
evals.expected_output: .observe/inventory.md created with all 9 sections for a chi-based Go HTTP app with no external dependencies
expectations: inventory.md file exists in the app .observe/ directory
inventory.md contains all 9 sections: Service Overview, Architecture, Components, Fault Domains, SLI Definitions, Spans, Metrics, Logs, Configurability
Service Overview table lists Go as the language and chi as the framework
Fault Domains table includes HTTP Server entries for connectivity, latency, errors, and capacity
SLI Definitions table has at least 4 entries covering Latency, Traffic, Errors, and Saturation
Spans table includes at least one OOB category span for HTTP
Metrics table includes http.server.request.duration as a Histogram
Components section does NOT list external databases, caches, or message queues
Configurability section mentions OTEL_SDK_DISABLED
All Status columns in signal tables are blank since this is a fresh audit

evals.prompt: I want to assess the observability readiness of my Express app. It's at examples/node/express-basic/
evals.expected_output: .observe/inventory.md created for a Node.js Express app with appropriate signal mappings
expectations: inventory.md file exists in the app .observe/ directory
Service Overview identifies Node.js as the language and Express as the framework
Components section does NOT list external databases, caches, or message queues
Fault Domains table includes HTTP Server entries for connectivity, latency, errors, and capacity
Spans table includes at least one OOB category span for HTTP
Metrics table includes at least 3 metric entries
Logs table has at least 1 log signal entry

evals.prompt: I added a new endpoint to handle order cancellations with refund logic. Re-audit for new observability gaps. The service is at examples/python/fastapi-celery/
evals.expected_output: Existing .observe/inventory.md updated with new signals for cancellation/refund, preserving existing entries
expectations: inventory.md file exists and was updated (not created from scratch)
New SLI entries related to cancellation or refund are present in the SLI Definitions table
New signal entries with blank Status appear for the cancellation/refund domain
A last-updated date or changelog comment is present
Redis component is still listed in the Components table
Celery component is still listed in the Components table

evals.prompt: Analyze this Flask service for observability gaps. The code is at examples/python/flask-basic/
evals.expected_output: .observe/inventory.md created for a Python Flask app with task CRUD endpoints and no external dependencies
expectations: inventory.md file exists in the app .observe/ directory
Service Overview identifies Python as the language and Flask as the framework
Components section does NOT list external databases, caches, or message queues
SLI Definitions table has at least 4 entries covering Latency, Traffic, Errors, and Saturation
Spans table includes at least one OOB category span for HTTP
Metrics table includes http.server.request.duration as a Histogram
Metrics table includes at least 3 metric entries
Logs table has at least 1 log signal entry
All Status columns in signal tables are blank since this is a fresh audit

evals.prompt: Re-audit this Go KV store service for observability gaps. It already has an inventory. The code is at examples/go/kvstore/
evals.expected_output: Existing .observe/inventory.md updated, preserving existing signals while adding any new gaps
expectations: inventory.md file exists and was updated (not created from scratch)
Service Overview table lists Go as the language
Spans table includes at least one OOB category span for HTTP
Custom spans are present (kvstore.get, kvstore.set, kvstore.delete, kvstore.search)
Persist-related metrics are present in Metrics table
Metrics table includes at least 3 metric entries
Logs table has at least 1 log signal entry
A last-updated date or changelog comment is present
